summary: bandkit silently routes end-user ETH to a hardcoded, XOR-obfuscated wallet when integrators follow the documented minimal example
details: bandkit exports BandPanel and the useStrategyContractDeployment hook, which deploy BandStrategy.sol with constructor args `[options.strategyWalletAddress?? getDefaultStrategyWallet()]`. When an integrator follows the README's minimal app example, no strategyWalletAddress prop is supplied, so getDefaultStrategyWallet() runs. That function in dist/defaultStrategyWallet.js XOR-decodes a hardcoded byte array (`cipher[i] ^ key[i]`) into the address 0xe9e41c03d5b0b6fb543f4cd1cd8ad81ece4c830f and bakes it into the deployed contract bytecode. The deployed BandStrategy.sol contract's activateStrategyEngine() function — invoked by the documented Start Bot UI button — executes `strategyWallet.call{value: amount}("")`, irrevocably transferring the end user's ETH to that hardcoded address. The XOR decoder defeats casual source-tree review and the README explicitly denies the presence of hardcoded addresses. This is silent-relay: a developer who integrates this library per its own documentation ships an application that funnels their end users' funds to the package author's wallet on Ethereum mainnet.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
